questions.question: Consider an auction where bidders have valuations of items drawn from a uniform distribution between 0 and 1. A bidder submits their valuation at the beginning of the auction. What is the expected revenue generated by the auctioneer in this setting?
questions.hint: You may assume that bidders are risk-neutral.

questions.question: In an auction where bidders submit their valuations in a sealed-bid format, the expected revenue of the auctioneer is maximised when the auctioneer sets a reserve price equal to the expected valuation of the item. Prove this result using the strategy-steering approach.
questions.hint: You may assume that bidders are risk-neutral and that the distribution of valuations is arbitrary.

questions.question: Suppose that the valuations of bidders in an auction are drawn from an arbitrary distribution. Show that the Vickrey-Clarke-Groves (VCG) auction always produces the socially optimal allocation of the item.
questions.hint: You may assume that the distribution of valuations is arbitrary and that the item is indivisible.
